title: Fiery Andalusian Beef Stew
description: A nutty, light one-pot Spanish stew with seared beef fillet and a subtle flame from sherry vinegar.
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.
Add diced onion and cook until translucent.
Stir in minced garlic and smoked paprika, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add beef fillet pieces, searing each side until brown.
Spoon in tomato paste and stir to coat the beef.
Pour in beef broth and sherry vinegar, bring to a gentle simmer.
Add cumin, cinnamon, salt and pepper, then cover and let cook for 45 minutes.
While the stew is simmering, toast pine nuts in a dry skillet until golden.
Once the stew has thickened slightly, stir in the toasted pine nuts.
Continue cooking uncovered for another 10 minutes to meld flavors.
Remove the pot from heat and let the stew rest for 5 minutes before serving hot.
